channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Cerebras falls 10% after chipmaker forecasts shrinking margin in first earnings report since IPO
item.description: Cerebras went public on the Nasdaq in May, giving Wall Street access to a pureplay AI company.

item.title: SpaceX raises $25 billion in debt sale less than two weeks after IPO
item.description: SpaceX raised $25 billion in a debt sale, after seeing nearly $90 billion worth of orders, sources say.

item.title: Alphabet added to Dow Jones Industrial Average, replacing Verizon
item.description: Alphabet will replace Verizon in the Dow Jones Industrial Average.

item.title: Factory job cuts in June neared financial crisis and Covid levels, S&P says
item.description: Though the firm's manufacturing index ran better than expected for June, it came largely from an inventory rebuild and despite sharp job cuts.

item.title: South Korea's stock market just had one of its worst days ever. Why Morgan Stanley says it's not breaking down
item.description: The Kospi index fell about 10% in Tuesday's session, marking its worst session since March.

item.title: FedEx posts strong earnings results in last quarter with freight business
item.description: FedEx posted strong fiscal fourth quarter earnings on Tuesday in the company's last quarter that included the freight business before its spin-off.

item.title: PGA Tour CEO Brian Rolapp unveils sweeping changes to professional golf
item.description: Changes to the PGA Tour are designed to elevate competition and raise payouts for winners.

item.title: House expected to pass affordable housing bill, send it to Trump's desk
item.description: The House on Tuesday could give final passage to a bill aimed at lowering costs for homebuyers and reining in private equity.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves after hours: FedEx, Cerebras, KB Home & more
item.description: See which stocks are posting big moves after the bell.

item.title: Senate backs Iran war powers resolution as GOP pressure grows on Trump's deal to end war
item.description: Senate Republicans want more details on Trump’s Iran deal as questions swirl around sanctions relief, nuclear restrictions and congressional approval.

item.title: Mamdani-backed candidates are likely to win in NYC primaries, prediction market traders expect
item.description: Voters are heading to the polls in New York, with four competitive races in Democratic congressional primaries in focus.

item.title: Five things to watch in Tuesday's primaries as New York races take center stage
item.description: Tuesday’s primaries in New York, Maryland and Utah test outside money, party power and the influence of Zohran Mamdani and Donald Trump.

item.title: Bill Gates testimony on Jeffrey Epstein ties released by House oversight panel
item.description: "I should never have met with Epstein in the first place," Bill Gates told members of the House Oversight and Government Reform Committee.

item.title: Micron leads the big tech sell-off. What the charts show about the next big catalyst
item.description: We can gain valuable insight when we look at longer-term trends that help us rise above the inevitable near-term noise in this secular AI-driven bull market.

item.title: CFTC sues Kentucky over actions against prediction markets, making it first red state to face federal scrutiny
item.description: The commission has now sued nine states in its battle to defend what it sees as its exclusive right to regulate event contracts.

item.title: Inside Kevin Warsh's selection process for the next Atlanta Fed president
item.description: Choosing a new head for the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta is an opportunity for new Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh to reshape the central bank.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz evacuation plan to begin for ships stranded in Persian Gulf, maritime organization says
item.description: More than 11,000 seafarers stuck in the Persian Gulf will begin to exit through the Hormuz Strait in a large-scale evacuation plan backed by Iran and the U.S.

item.title: Traders are loving this cheap way to make big bets against chip stocks
item.description: Just a day after making new all-time records, the semiconductor sector is down almost 7% and traders are finding a cheap way to bet on a bigger pivot.

item.title: Meta announces new smart glasses starting at $299, as Zuckerberg keeps pushing wearables
item.description: Meta executives have said they see the lightweight smart glasses as a step towards a more advanced device that includes screens in the lenses.

item.title: Meta is building a prediction markets app. These stocks fell in response
item.description: The report said that CEO Mark Zuckerberg directed staff to develop a prediction markets platform, internally known as "Arena."

item.title: An off-grid power project gets a major proof of concept. What it means for GE Vernova
item.description: AI data centers powered by off-grid power plants took a major step forward this week, a good sign for GE Vernova as industry challenges pile up.

item.title: 5 best credit unions for auto loans in July 2026
item.description: Credit unions often offer lower interest rates on car loans and tend to be more flexible when working with borrowers who have poor credit.

item.title: Could there be a $10 trillion company? Tech investing star Laffont thinks so
item.description: A new regime of trillion-dollar companies is replacing the 'Magnificent Seven.'

item.title: Sen. Cassidy plans to push 'big idea' for Social Security reform in last days in office
item.description: As Social Security faces an imminent funding shortfall, Sen. Bill Cassidy said he has come up with a plan to invest in the stock market on the program's behalf.

item.title: The big questions the bull market faces right now as the S&P 500 hits some turbulence
item.description: Mike Santoli addresses the big questions facing the stock market right now.

item.title: Progressive Brad Lander’s surging bid for Congress splinters Democrats' labor union alliance
item.description: Rep. Dan Goldman, D-N.Y., is fighting for his political life in a race with Brad Lander, who is backed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani and Sen. Bernie Sanders, I-Vt.

item.title: SpaceX seeing some interest from short sellers, but many still afraid to bet against Musk
item.description: About 40 million SpaceX shares are sold short, roughly 5% to 7% of the shares that are publicly available to trade, according to an estimate by S3 Partners.

item.title: SpaceX closes nearly 1% higher, snapping three-day losing streak
item.description: Gains have been pared back at the space and AI company following an initial surge off of its record-breaking IPO.

item.title: U.S. issues sweeping Iran oil sanctions waivers, unlocking billions in revenue for Tehran
item.description: The U.S. issued a sweeping rollback of sanctions on Iranian oil, offering Tehran an economic lifeline as negotiations toward a peace deal proceed.

item.title: 'I like their money': Trump threatens lawsuits against ABC for reporting on Reflecting Pool
item.description: The latest action against ABC comes as the broadcaster faces two investigations from the Federal Communications Commission.
